pattern: The first n odd numbers sum to a perfect square and the first n even numbers sum to a product, so the answer reduces to a single greatest-common-divisor of two closed-form totals.
hints: You do not need to build the two lists; each sum has a tidy formula in terms of n.
The first n odd numbers add to n*n, and the first n even numbers add to n*(n+1).
Compute both totals from n and return their gcd; note gcd(n*n, n*(n+1)) always works out to n itself, but computing the gcd directly is safest.

edge_inputs.description: mid value to catch a wrong formula

stress.description: random n across the full range
